column1: #Text=The thrush nightingale, also known as the sprosser, is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family.
#Text=It is a migratory species breeding in forests in Europe and the Palearctic and overwintering in Africa.
#Text=An insectivore, it nests near the ground in dense undergrowth.
#Text=Its distribution is more northerly than the very closely related common nightingale, which it closely resembles in appearance.
#Text=Hybrids between the two have occurred in areas where their ranges overlap.
